name.en: Cliff Temple Viewpoint (Wat Pha Tak Suea)

address: Wat Pha Tak Suea, Pha Tang subdistrict, Sangkhom District, Nong Khai
opening_hours: Temple and skywalk access are generally daytime-focused; recheck local opening and weather conditions before setting out.

description.en: The clearest Mekong-cliff viewpoint on the Sangkhom side, pairing a temple setting with one of Nong Khai province's best panoramas.
description.nl: Het duidelijkste Mekong-klifuitzicht aan de Sangkhom-kant, waar een tempelsetting samengaat met een van de beste panorama's van de provincie Nong Khai.

highlights: Wat Pha Tak Suea viewpoint logic
Strongest headline stop on the Sangkhom side

metaDescription.en: Why the cliff temple viewpoint at Wat Pha Tak Suea is the strongest Sangkhom-side panorama from Nong Khai.
metaDescription.nl: Waarom het kliftempel-uitzichtpunt bij Wat Pha Tak Suea het sterkste Sangkhom-panorama vanuit Nong Khai is.

enhanced_description:
This entry is best read as the broader viewpoint logic around Wat Pha Tak Suea rather than as a separate attraction from the skywalk itself. The cliff edge, temple setting, and Mekong outlook are what make the stop strong; the exact platform is only one part of the experience.

That is why it remains one of the clearest high-value province add-ons from Nong Khai city. If you want one decisive Sangkhom-side panorama, this is usually the one.
detailed_history: Current TAT route material for Nong Khai still presents Wat Pha Tak Suea as a Sangkhom-side highlight with sweeping Mekong views, while guidebook material continues to treat the cliff-edge temple and skywalk together as the province's best-known panorama stop.
visitor_experience: Expect a viewpoint-first stop with some temple atmosphere around it. Weather and visibility matter more than trying to stretch the visit into a long half-day temple experience.

practical_info.how_to_get_there: Best reached by road as part of a Sangkhom-side outing from Nong Khai city.
practical_info.nearby_attractions: Den Ping Cave, Phu Huai Isan, and other Mekong river-road stops in Sangkhom District.
practical_info.food_options: Eat before or after the viewpoint run rather than expecting the stop itself to carry the meal plan.
practical_info.shopping: Minimal; this is a viewpoint stop.

insider_tips: Treat this as the main panorama stop if you only do one Sangkhom-side outing.
Do not overload the same day with too many weaker viewpoints afterward.
Check local conditions if wind, rain, or mist matters to your visit.
verified_note: Source-backed summary based on current TAT route material and official guidebook coverage for Nong Khai province.
cultural_significance: The cliff temple viewpoint matters because it connects Nong Khai's religious landscape to the Mekong border geography in one very legible stop.

contentSources.name: 3D2N to Udon Thani and Nong Khai For Beliefs and Beyond
